item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was hilarious, sweet, and heartwarming. There is not a lot of scps that actually make you feel good inside when you read it, Mostly its just things that may be cute or may not rip your face off if it gets the chance. You have a story of sorts and its just really well written. I hope to see more of your articles in time(trust me if there is another I either have read it or will)

take my +1 with some marshmallows on top

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article was missing the

« SCP-2982 | SCP-2983 | SCP-2984 »

I added it in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fact that the Foundation set aside 120 million USD to get the prize on the side of a cereal box is hilarious. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have not much to say to this except that you, my good sir, are an exceptionally respectable dude.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Here is a link to the Wayback Machine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Saw this, and couldn't leave it alone.

I think we’ll try to write some of this stuff in to earn your upvote

My upvote is entirely immaterial, don't put too much stock in it. The important thing is producing the best possible version of whatever it is we're putting forth. I'm more concerned with whether or not my critique is serviceable to authors, or useful to the readership, which, in this case, it thankfully appears to be.

One should not be making changes to earn an arbitrary extra digit or two to one's vote total, but when they are in agreement that there are fixable flaws.

That being said, at a passing glance, there's been enough work on this to where I feel as though I can neutral vote for the time being - I voted on the article as it was earlier, not on it as it is now. I'll reread this again with fresh eyes in the coming days. Kudos for the quick work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Out of curiosity, what was the original SCP-2983?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the feedback! We've fixed it up significantly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is pretty comprehensible, implementable critique. I think we’ll try to write some of this stuff in to earn your upvote, thank you for the comment. Also:

Doubly happy that the newer take on Wondertainment is seeing presence in the mainlist.

Me too man, me too <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Had this ended with the log of 'mallows, I would have been tempted to upvote. It's a cute base idea with a flair of detail to help bring it to life, and the references in the table are an example of good, fun, crosslinking (and bonus points for the handling of Headless!). Doubly happy that the newer take on Wondertainment is seeing presence in the mainlist.

However, the story has a few elements that, taken altogether, make it an unsatisfying read overall. It feels like some parts are introduced without much thought in how they fit into the whole.

+ show block
– hide block

First, and this is something I'm typically not pendantic about, but Safe class being used for something that requires ongoing, active monitoring and retrieval - something that can appear with little forewarning in public places - doesn't fit with how that class is typically used.

Second, while the first set of crosslinks is sensible (and good product placement!), it doesn't make a lick of sense to link 3203. In the context of that article, it is not even formally recognized as an SCP, and beyond that, it's something the higher-ups try to brush under the rug; it's not exactly something I can imagine the O5s calling attention to elsewhere in the database. I would try to find something more appropriate (perhaps Mr Money? that'd be keeping in with the rest of the references, but I cannot recall if his ability is to actually generate money off the top of my head).

Third, it's a little strange from an in-universe perspective for there to have been no mention of earlier prizes showing up prior to the final log, almost as an aside. Did they all appear at once? If so, It doesn't feel sensible for the first prize to not have manifested prior to the release of the other cereals. It feels like there's a hole in progression.

Finally, and most importantly: The sudden introduction of Waters at the ass-end of the article makes the final message ring hollow. It feels entirely arbitrary for the prizes to be directed at her, considering that she has no presence elsewhere in the work. The easiest fix, I feel, to give it a bit more coherence, would be to simply edit her in earlier on, replacing stuff like "research team". Or mention her as the research head or somesuch.

Ideally though, I think she should have more prominence than that. Minor, clinical updates and addenda and proposals (like the O5 vote) could instead be infused with her voice, making her a character in her own right instead of a one-off vehicle for resolution. This should help the ending feel more natural, and earned.

-1, it's not quite as strong as it can be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sweets & Consumables Department = SCD = Secure Contain Defraud

i think I cracked the code y'all

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this made me feel warm and fuzzy. it also made me want to buy large amounts of solely cereal marshmallows.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good job my dudes, I enjoyed it.i did not write this piece i just forgot to make a new discussion post

item.title: *wondertainment brand confetti canon*
item.content:encoded:
Man I’m really tired so I can’t do this post justice right now but I’d feel bad leaving nothing so. Really happy to have collaborated with Uncle Nicolini, really happy to have finally posted a Wobdertainment skip (especially one that connects to my canon), and… yeah. Happy about this. Judy’s my baby and I’m always happy to write her more.

Alternate titles:

  • She’s a Good Egg
  • How the Foundation Accidentally Made the World a Slightly Better Place
Some art I did
Hope you like it

item.title: *party horn*
item.content:encoded:
I've wanted to colab with DarkStuff for a while, and it finally happened! I first approached him with a very bare-bones version of this article which only had a weird singing and dancing cereal made by Wondertainment, but I'm glad he was able to expand it to be more with me.
Love ya, buddy.

Also, love the critters!
Thank you Meserach, stormbreath, Weryllium, Gabriel Jade, Stallmantic, 9Volt, and Captain Kirby.
